rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY KWONG VS. JOHN DENNY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Prior Order Denying Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Tradition Mandamus (Code Civ. Proc. 1085) Regarding The 12th To 19th Causes Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Thursday, July 18, 2018, Line 2, PETITIONER JEFFREY KWONG Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Prior Order Denying Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Tradition Mandamus (Code Civ. Proc. 1085) Regarding The 12th To 19th Causes Of Action Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER IS DENIED. PETITIONER PRESENTS NO NEW EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT RECONSIDERATION AND THE COURT BELIEVES THAT ITS PRIOR ORDER WAS CORRECT. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative =(302/JPT)

Proc. 2033.280, Subds. (B) And (C) On The Respondent City And County Of San Francisco Or Respondents Attorney, Margaret W. Baumgartner, State Bar #151762, Or Both For Failure To Respond To An Authorized Method Of Discovery, Constituting A Misuse Of The Discovery Process. (Code Civ. Proc. 2023.010, Subds. (D) Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PETITIONERS MOTION FOR AN ORDER THAT HIS REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION BE DEEMED ADMITED IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: (1) DEFENDANTS' GENERAL OBJECTIONS PARAGRAPHS 3 - 6 ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO PROVIDE PETITIOINER WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOS 5 (INITIAL RESPONSE IS NOT RESPONSIVE TO THE REQUEST), 7 (NOT REPONSIVE TO REQUEST) & 8 (N0 RESPONSE). FAILURE TO DO SO WILL RESULT IN THESE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION TO BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND DEFENDANTS' WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM INTRODUCING ANY EVIDENCE CHALLENGING THE ADMISSION. (2) EXCEPT AS SET FORTH IN PARAGRAPH (1) ABOVE, PETITIONERS MOTION TO HAVE THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED IS DENIED (3) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $512.75 AGAINST DEFENDANTS TO REIMBURSE PETITIONER FOR COSTS INCURRED, PAYMENT OF WHICHSHALL BE MADE TO PETITIONER WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY KWONG VS. JOHN DENNY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Jeffrey Kwong'S Motion For Order To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set No. 1) And To Special Interrogatories (Set No. 1) And For Monetary Sanctions Of $360.40 On The Respondent City And County Of San Francisco, Or Attorney Margaret W. Baumgartner, State Bar #151762, Or Both For Failure To Respond To Authorized Methods Of Discovery And For Misuse Of The Discovery Process Under Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 2030.290 And 2023.010
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday July 20, 2018, Line 2, Petitioner Jeffrey Kwong'S Motion For Order To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set No. 1) And To Special Interrogatories (Set No. 1) And For Monetary Sanctions Of $360.40 On The Respondent City And County Of San Francisco, Or Attorney Margaret W. Baumgartner, State Bar #151762, Or Both For Failure To Respond To Authorized Methods Of Discovery And For Misuse Of The Discovery Process Under Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 2030.290 And 2023.010. Part 1 of 2 Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: CONTENTION THAT THIS COURT'S EARLIER ORDER MAKES MOOT ANY FURTHER DISCOVERY IN THIS MATTER. THAT CONTENTION IGNORES THE FACT THAT PERTITIONER'S PETITION SEEKS RELIEF IN ADDITION TO MANDAMUS AND HE HAS A RIGHT TO PURSUE THOSE CLAIMS UNTIL A JUDGMENT IS ENTERED EITHER GRANTING OR DISMISSING THE CLAIM FOR RELIEF. THE EARLIER ORDER DEALT SOLELY TO MOTIONS BROUGHT BY PETITIONER ADDRESS TO HIS CLAIMS UNDER CCP 1085, 1086 AND 1094.6. PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE) AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE) IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: (1) DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AS SET FORTH IN PARAGRAPHS 2 & 3 OF ITS GENERAL OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO PROVIDE PPETITIONER WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER WITH FURTHER RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4,12.5, 12.6,12.7, 14.1, 15.1, & 17.1 FOR ANY RESPONSE TO THE REQUEST FOR ADMISSION WHERE THE ANSWER WAS NOT AN UNQUALIFIED ADMISSION (RFA NOS. 2, 3, 4,7, 9, 11, 12, 15, 1718 & 19),. THE REFERENCE TO THE "ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD DOES NOT ADEQUATELY RESPOND TO THE SPECIFIC INFORMATION SOUGHT BY THE INTERROGATORIES. (2) DEFENDANTS'OBJECTIONS TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AS SET FORTH IN PARAGRAPHS 3 - 6 ARE OVERRULED AND DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO PROVIDE PETITIONER WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER FURTHER RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 2, 3 (IF THE INTERROGATORIES IS ACCURATE IN STATING DEFENDANTS' CONTENTION), 4, 5 & 6 (IF THE INTRROGATORY IS ACCURATE IN STATING DEFENDANTS; CONTENTION. (3) PETITIONER'S MOTION IS DENIED AS TO ANY FORM INTERROGATORY OR SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO REFERRED TO ABOVE IN (1) & (2)., (4) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $360.00 AGAINST DEFENDANTS TO REIMBURSE PETITIONER FOR COSTS INCURRED, PAYMENT OF WHICH SHALL BE MADE TO PETITIONER WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER.(tentative ruling continued in part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL BOREEN VS. CLERK OF THE SUPERIOR COURT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion On Petition For Mandatory And Prohibitory Writ Of Mandate To Compel Ministerial Duty Of The Clerk Of The Superior Court For Entry Of Default Against Real Pearty In Interest Barrett Daffin Frappier Treder & Weiss, Llp
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Friday, July 20, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER DANIEL BOREEN'S Notice And Motion On Petition For Mandatory And Prohibitory Writ Of Mandate To Compel Ministerial Duty Of The Clerk Of The Superior Court For Entry Of Default Against Real Pearty In Interest Barrett Daffin Frappier Treder & Weiss, Llp. Boreen's petition for writ of mandate is DENIED. The clerk did not have a ministerial duty to enter the default under the circumstances of this case. The filing of the Declaration of Non-Monetary Status ("DNMS") on May 2, 2018 and Boreen's objection, which the court will assume was timely for purposes of this motion, tolled the time period for Barrett, Daffin, Frappier, Tredder & Weiss, LLP ("BDFTW") to respond to the complaint. Even if the filing of the second DNMS was improper, the clerk had no ability to enter the default. Determining the propriety of the filing of the second DNMS is a judicial function. (See Todd v. Everett (1966) 247 Cal.App.2d 209, 211.) The clerk had no authority to strike or ignore the DNMS and enter the default in light of CC 2924l(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for BDFTW is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: GARY KOVACS VS. AVG TECHNOLOGIES USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Gary Kovacs' Motion For Sanctions Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 2023.030 For Violations Of The April 2, 2018 Discovery Order
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Friday, July 20, 2018, Line 9, Plaintiff Gary Kovacs' Motion For Sanctions Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 2023.030 For Violations Of The April 2, 2018 Discovery Order. Judge Ulmer will hear this matter at 9:00 a.m. Judge Ulmer issues the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is DENIED. Rather than seek production of the few documents still at issue, plaintiff seeks issue sanctions that are effectively case-dispositive or sweeping evidentiary sanctions. Both proposed sets of sanctions are extreme, draconian and unwarranted. No monetary sanctions are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The order shall reflect that the amended complaint will be filed as the court does not deem pleadings filed. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA ** BENJAMIN ROSSINI VS. GLOBAL GOURMET CATERING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Friday, July 20, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT AVATAR FOOD INT'L, INC.'s Notice And Motion To Change Venue. Defendant Avatar Food International, Inc.'s motion to change venue based upon the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice is GRANTED. The court transfers this action to Santa Clara County and Avatar shall pay all required transfer fees. In determining whether the ends of justice will be promoted in transferring an action, "[t]he trial court may rely not only on the direct facts set forth in the affidavits, but also on any reasonable and relevant inference arising therefrom." (J.C. Millett Co. v. Latchford-Marble Glass Co. (1959) 167 Cal.App.2d 218, 227.) Here, the undisputed Roberts declaration demonstrates that the accident occurred in Santa Clara County, plaintiff received his medical care in Santa Clara County, and the primary investigators of the accident are located in Santa Clara County. The court can reasonably infer that most, if not all, of the key non-party witnesses are located at or near that county. In addition, Contreras v. Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. (18CV328362) is a related case that arises from the same accident. A transfer is necessary for potential consolidation of the two matters should the Santa Clara County Court so order. Consolidation may well preserve judicial and party resources and prevent duplicative work and inconsistent rulings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Avatar is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The order shall reflect that the amended complaint will be filed as the court does not deem pleadings filed. =(302/RBU)
